Open AutoPose v1.0.0 - Local Quick Guide

Local Vite hosting, offline runtime files, and browser-based pose/depth control workflows

Open AutoPose converts your own video clips into pose skeletons, silhouettes, depth maps, and control exports. The application is hosted locally from this folder. Videos remain on your machine unless you deliberately send them elsewhere.

Requirements

  • Node.js 20.19+ or 22.12+
  • Current Chrome or Microsoft Edge for WebGPU and WebCodecs features
  • Optional: FFmpeg and ffprobe on PATH for the CLI media tools
  • Your own commercially cleared video clip; no demonstration video is bundled

First-time setup

  1. Open a terminal in the extracted release folder.
  2. Install the exact locked JavaScript dependencies:
npm ci
AI models load on first use: the release bundles its application source and locked runtime, but it does not redistribute large third-party AI model weights. The first time you run pose tracking or Consistent depth, the local host downloads the pinned model files from their upstream sources, caches them under dist/vendor/, and reuses them offline on every later launch. Only that first model-using launch needs internet.

Development mode

npm run dev

Open http://127.0.0.1:5173. Do not double-click app.html or open it through file://; browser modules and WASM should be served over local HTTP.

Windows users can alternatively run Start_App.bat. macOS/Linux users can run ./Start_App.sh.

Production build and offline local host

npm run build
npm run serve

Open http://127.0.0.1:4173. The generated static site is in dist/. The included host binds to loopback only, so it is not exposed to other devices by default.

One-command production start: npm run start builds the current source and then hosts dist/.

Basic workflow

  1. Drag your video onto the source panel or click to browse.
  2. Select one or more characters and choose the desired pose model.
  3. Choose Track to generate body landmarks.
  4. Enable Skeleton, Silhouette, Fast Depth, or Consistent VDA Depth.
  5. Set In/Out points when you only need part of the clip.
  6. Export MP4, image sequences, pose JSON, OpenPose JSON, BVH, or bones JSON.

Layers

  • Skeleton: white or OpenPose-style body joints and bones.
  • Silhouette: body masks with white, black, green, magenta, or per-character tinting.
  • Fast depth: per-frame monocular depth with optional stabilization and smoothing.
  • Consistent VDA depth: temporally aware depth intended for stable sequences; requires supported WebGPU hardware.
  • Edges and pseudo-normals: additional ControlNet-oriented preprocessing layers.

Editing and multi-person tools

  • Drag joint handles to correct a frame manually.
  • Save and reuse corrected poses with the pose library.
  • Apply temporal smoothing or propagate selected edits over nearby frames.
  • Track up to five characters and customize each character color.
  • Use markers, compare mode, solo controls, presets, and project files for repeatable work.

Exports and command-line conversion

  • MP4: browser-produced control video when WebCodecs is supported.
  • PNG/JPEG/TIFF: numbered image sequences.
  • JSON/OpenPose: frame-by-frame pose keypoints.
  • BVH/bones JSON: approximate motion data for downstream 3D tools.
npm run frames -- --help
node api/cli/tp-frames-cli.mjs convert ./input.mp4 --out ./output.webm --preset webm

Local API gateway

npm run gateway

The gateway defaults to 127.0.0.1:8787. It refuses a non-loopback bind unless TP_API_TOKEN is set. Leave it on loopback unless remote API access is intentionally required.

Verification commands

npm test
npm run smoke
npm run probe
npm run test:all
  • npm test runs the unit and release-contract tests.
  • npm run smoke runs the offline smoke battery.
  • npm run probe checks portable browser capabilities and app-shell loading.
  • npm run test:all also builds the release and boots the module-loaded local app.

Install Playwright Chromium for portable automated browser testing when no suitable system Chrome/Edge is available:

npm run setup:test-browser

Troubleshooting

  • Quick Process or depth fails on first use: the first model-using launch needs internet to download the weights - check the connection and retry; they are cached for offline use afterwards.
  • Re-download models: delete the files under dist/vendor/models (and dist/vendor/vda) and rerun; they are fetched again on next use.
  • Consistent depth is unavailable: update Chrome/Edge and the GPU driver, or use Fast depth.
  • MP4 export is unavailable: use a current WebCodecs-capable browser or export an image sequence and convert it with FFmpeg.
  • Tracking misses a person / empty skeleton: in the Tracking panel, lower the detection slider (higher recall), make sure overlay gate is low and "Hide low-confidence joints" is off (so tracked joints are drawn), and try the Heavy pose-model tier. Also use a tighter crop and improve subject visibility.
  • Stylized / anime / illustrated characters: both shipped detection engines are trained on photographic humans, so detection on drawn characters is inherently limited. In the Tracking panel try switching the engine between MediaPipe (default) and ViTPose (experimental) - they fail differently, so one often finds a usable skeleton where the other does not. ViTPose is single-subject and 2D only, and its first run needs internet once to cache the model, then runs offline. Also enable Re-detect every frame, lower detection toward 0.1-0.2, and expect to finish sparse frames with manual keyframing in the editor. (DWPose is listed but disabled - it is coming once it can be validated.)
  • The page is blank after a source edit: stop the server, rerun npm ci, then restart npm run dev.

Before selling or publishing

  • Verify the app on your real target hardware before distributing.
  • Confirm WebGPU depth, WebCodecs export, and webcam behavior in current Chrome and Edge.
  • Review THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md and keep all required license notices with the product.
  • Only distribute media for which you hold the required commercial and privacy rights.

Open AutoPose v1.0.0. Runtime packages are installed from exact versions in package-lock.json. Optional model files are downloaded locally from their documented upstream sources. See README.md, INSTALL.md, and THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md.
